name: Relic Synergy
description: An interaction pattern where two or more run items, relics, cards, or modifiers produce stronger, altered, or newly enabled behavior together.
design_notes: Relic synergy is a buildcraft promise: players learn item rules, spot combinations, and feel clever when effects reinforce each other. The implementation should avoid hidden order dependence by defining trigger events, stack limits, priority, and debug traces for each synergy calculation.
design_purpose: Encourage players to draft around themes, tags, and payoff effects.
Make repeated runs feel different through emergent combinations.
Reward knowledge without requiring every synergy to be explicitly scripted.
player_fantasy: The player feels their collected relics have become a coherent engine rather than isolated bonuses.

parameters.name: relic_instance_id
parameters.description: Run-scoped relic instance participating in synergy checks.

parameters.name: synergy_tag_id
parameters.description: Shared tag that links relics, triggers, and payoff effects.

parameters.name: trigger_event_id
parameters.description: Event that evaluates or activates the synergy.

parameters.name: effect_priority_order
parameters.description: Deterministic ordering for simultaneous relic effects.

parameters.name: synergy_cap_rule_id
parameters.description: Limiter that prevents runaway loops or unreadable scaling.

parameters.name: debug_reason_trace
parameters.typical_range: effect id and contributing relic ids
parameters.description: Designer-readable explanation of why a synergy fired.

edge_cases: Two relics trigger each other recursively and create an infinite event loop.
A relic is removed or transformed while another relic is iterating over active instances.
Synergy tags change after an upgrade and invalidate cached tooltip text.
Networked or replayed runs resolve simultaneous relic events in different orders.
A curse or modifier suppresses one relic but the synergy UI still lists it as active.
common_bugs: Effect order follows collection order and changes after save/load sorting.
Stack caps apply per relic definition instead of per relic_instance_id, breaking duplicate behavior.
Tooltips advertise a synergy from tags that are not eligible in the current room or mode.
Recursive triggers are blocked globally and accidentally prevent legitimate chained effects.
Debug output only reports final stats, not the contributing relics or trigger_event_id.
balancing_notes: Strong synergies are memorable, but every build archetype needs enough counterweights and dead-end prevention.
Explicit pair synergies are easier to tune; tag-based synergies scale better but need caps and telemetry.
Some anti-synergy is healthy when it creates draft tension, but invisible anti-synergy feels like a bug.
accessibility_notes: Expose synergy previews and active combo explanations in tooltips or a run log.
Avoid relying on players remembering item names from earlier rooms; show current contributing relics.
Use text and icons for synergy tags so color-coded archetypes remain readable.

implementation_notes.unity: Model relic definitions as data assets and relic_instance_id values as run state. Route trigger_event_id through a deterministic event bus, sort effects by effect_priority_order, and record debug_reason_trace for tooltips and bug reports.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep relic instances in an ordered run-state collection and use signals for trigger events. Guard recursive emissions with synergy_cap_rule_id and recompute UI tooltip text after relic transforms or curse suppression.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Gameplay Tags for synergy_tag_id and a replicated component for active relic instances. Resolve simultaneous triggers on the authority side, then replicate resulting effects and debug tags for client presentation.
implementation_notes.web: Implement synergy resolution as a pure reducer over ordered relic instances and event records. Store effect priority, stack counts, and cap counters in run state so refreshes and replays produce the same chain.

relationships.reason: Relic synergies give random powerups build identity and encourage long-term run planning.
Curses can counterbalance strong relic combinations with constraints that affect build decisions.
Relic effects can modify combo windows, rewards, or on-hit triggers for action roguelike builds.

example_games.title: Slay the Spire
example_games.note: Uses relic and card interactions as a major buildcraft layer.

example_games.title: The Binding of Isaac
example_games.note: Uses item combinations that can transform run behavior.
